stories.headline: Iran launches more strikes after accusing US of striking near nuclear plant
stories.summary: Centcom says it hit 90 Iranian targets in the latest round of strikes, with the Iranian health ministry saying 14 people were killed since Tuesday.

stories.headline: U.S. Begins Second Day of Strikes Against Iran
stories.summary: President Trump said earlier that a cease-fire was over and warned that the United States would probably hit Iran “hard.”

stories.headline: International court tells BBC of breakthrough in Sudan war crimes probe
stories.summary: The ICC has been looking into atrocities committed in Darfur over the past three years.

stories.headline: Sharp drop in ships crossing Strait of Hormuz after US and Iran trade strikes
stories.summary: Data shows a decline in the number of ships - many carrying oil and gas - going through the waterway after attacks this week.

stories.headline: Democrat Graham Platner suspends campaign for key US Senate race after assault allegation
stories.summary: The decision came days after a woman accused him of sexual assault, an accusation he says is "categorically false".

stories.headline: US, Iran Trade Airstrikes as Fears Grow of a Return to War
stories.summary: The US military attacked Iran for a second day and Tehran retaliated against American allies in the Persian Gulf, raising fears of a return to war after little progress in efforts to secure a diplomatic outcome.

stories.headline: Graham Platner drops Maine Senate bid: What that means for Democrats
stories.summary: Democrats are scrambling to field a candidate as popular as Platner to clinch Maine in the midterms.

stories.headline: US strikes Iran for second night – is the peace process all over now?
stories.summary: Neither side has officially called off the interim peace agreement, despite the escalation.

stories.headline: UN probe finds mass killings, gang rapes by Sudan’s RSF amount to genocide
stories.summary: UN Fact-Finding Mission found that the paramilitary's systematic campaign of violence in Darfur amounted to genocide.

stories.headline: Ukraine strikes Russian ships near Crimea, escalating attacks on fuel supplies
stories.summary: The attacks appear to be the latest phase of Ukraine's bid to choke off supplies and routes into and out of occupied Crimea.

stories.headline: Flexport CEO Warns Shipping Risks Remain
stories.summary: Flexport CEO Ryan Petersen joins Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why the biggest threat to global shipping isn't the Strait of Hormuz—it's still the Red Sea. He breaks down the surge in imports driven by tariff uncertainty, why companies are stockpiling inventory after the COVID supply chain crisis, and how Panama Canal drought risks could create the next bottleneck. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Graham Platner Suspended His Senate Campaign. Here’s What’s Next for Democrats.
stories.summary: The deadline to pick a new nominee is July 27 and candidates are already lining up. State party leaders said they would hold some form of nominating convention.

stories.headline: Inside the Implosion of Graham Platner’s Maine Senate Campaign: ‘A Slow-Rolling Disaster’
stories.summary: Graham Platner’s bid for the Senate inspired progressive Democrats. But the campaign, which he suspended Wednesday, was messy, disorganized and ultimately doomed by a steady drip of scandal.

stories.headline: Platner Suspends Senate Bid in Maine After Rape Accusation
stories.summary: Mr. Platner’s withdrawal, which comes after a woman accused him of rape, creates deep uncertainty in a race that both parties see as crucial to their hopes of winning the Senate.

stories.headline: Democrats Grow Frustrated as Graham Platner Resists Dropping Out Quickly in Maine
stories.summary: Two days after a woman accused Graham Platner of rape, he had not given up his Senate nomination in Maine, even as state Democrats announced plans to hold a nominating convention if he steps down.

stories.headline: Barcelona registers highest temperature in 112 years as French nuclear reactor shuts down due to extreme heat – as it happened
stories.summary: Some stations in Spain recorded temperatures of up to 44C after western Europe experienced the warmest June on recordA high temperature warning has also been issued for a dozen counties in the Republic of Ireland, PA reported.Irish national forecaster Met Éireann said temperatures could exceed 27C during the day with overnight temperatures staying above 15C. The agency warned of water safety issues, heat stress, and uncomfortable sleeping conditi

stories.headline: Deadly US strikes trigger Iranian attacks on Gulf states
stories.summary: The US military says it has struck 90 targets in Iran. Iran says it sent drone strikes on US-linked sites in the Gulf.

stories.headline: Australia, India strike deal on uranium exports during PM Modi’s visit
stories.summary: India has long eyed Australia's uranium to help meet a target of 100 gigawatts of nuclear energy capacity by 2047.

stories.headline: NATO summit ended 'on a more positive note than feared', former NATO security advisor says
stories.summary: Jamie Shea, former NATO security adviser, says NATO 3.0 is going to be "much more a European show and less an American show" than it used to be. According to Shea, the US "is going to do a lot less in terms of holding up the collective defence than allies were used to during the Cold War".

stories.headline: Factory fire kills at least 28 in China's 'shoe capital'
stories.summary: Footage shows huge flames rising from a building and thick, black smoke rising into the sky.

stories.headline: US strikes Iranian railway bridges on route to city of Khamenei’s burial
stories.summary: Late supreme leader set to be interred in Mashhad on Thursday

stories.headline: Brazil Miner Vale Facing Scrutiny Over Chair’s Exit Compensation
stories.summary: Vale SA, the world’s top iron ore producer, is under scrutiny by Brazil’s securities regulator following media reports that this week’s resignation of its board chair included a deal for a compensation package.

stories.headline: Second Person in a Week Killed by Federal Task Force in Memphis
stories.summary: Tennessee authorities are investigating both shootings involving agents working with the task force in Memphis.

stories.headline: Trump’s Mixed Messages at NATO Summit
stories.summary: As the NATO Summit in Ankara, Turkey, comes to an end, Tyler Pager, The Times’s White House correspondent, explains how Trump lashed out at European NATO allies while also declaring unity.

stories.headline: Australia and India seal uranium export pact, boost defense ties
Thai court greenlights government's $12bn emergency borrowing
Russia fuel crisis spills over to Central Asia as drone strikes bite
Japan PM misses chance for European diplomacy by skipping NATO summit
SK hynix: From near-collapse to a $1 trillion valuation and a Nasdaq listing
stories.summary: SK hynix, the South Korean chipmaker whose memory chips power AI data centres around the world, lists on the Nasdaq this week in one of the largest share sales in history, capping a journey from near-bankruptcy to a trillion-dollar valuation.

stories.headline: Who is Count Binface – the alien space warrior standing against Brexit champion Nigel Farage?
stories.summary: In a week when the race to become Britain’s next prime minister is effectively a one-horse race, there seems to be more suspense in another contest pitting Brexit champion Nigel Farage against a comedian going viral online. Is this a joke too far for Brits and their famous sense of humour?

stories.headline: The century-old device choking the world’s AI push
stories.summary: Surging data centre power demands are intensifying pressure on transformer supply chains

stories.headline: European heatwave damps sales growth at clothing retailer Uniqlo

stories.headline: Regulator to block CME bid to fast-track round-the-clock oil contracts

stories.headline: CME launches new product to simplify controversial Treasury bets
stories.summary: Instrument set to cut cost of popular hedge fund ‘basis trade’ strategy that has been blamed for adding to market volatility

stories.headline: Merz’s Reforms for Germany Get Fitch Cheer But No Big Applause
stories.summary: Germany’s new reform package amounts to a step in the right direction but is still no game changer in addressing the troubles of its economy, according to Fitch Ratings.

stories.headline: France Launches Plan to Cut Fertilizer Import Reliance
stories.summary: France will support a €2 billion ($2.3 billion) industrial investment program to cut dependence on costly fertilizer imports in an effort to shield farmers from future supply disruptions.

stories.headline: Security Precaution Led Trump to Use Old Air Force One in Leaving Turkey
stories.summary: The Secret Service is said to have asked that the president not use the Qatari-donated jet when he left Ankara. The swap deepens questions about the retrofitting of the new plane.

stories.headline: US and Iran trade strikes after tankers hit in Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: The US military says it hit missile sites and command centres while Iran says it attacked US bases.

stories.headline: Trump takes aim at 'wasted cause' Spain and revives Greenland claim at Nato summit
stories.summary: The US president lashes out at fellow Nato member Spain, repeating an earlier threat to cut off trade relations.

stories.headline: U.S. and Iran launch fresh strikes following attacks on commercial ships
stories.summary: Iran said it had targeted more than 80 U.S. military facilities in Bahrain and Kuwait, after the U.S. launched strikes in response to attacks on commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Trump threatens more strikes on Iran as Tehran warns of 'fearless' response
stories.summary: Speaking at a Nato summit, the president says the US will "probably" strike Iran again but added that "anything that happens will be over quickly".

stories.headline: Cubans protest after third nationwide power cut this year
stories.summary: While Cuban officials said that power had been mostly restored, those still left in the dark vented their anger.

stories.headline: Russian fuel shortages bite – but will Putin change tack in Ukraine war?
stories.summary: Even in Moscow, authorities are unable to guarantee fuel supplies, but will economic pressure lead to talks or escalation?

stories.headline: A mountain of rubbish in Indonesia has been on fire for more than a week
stories.summary: Environmental activists say the blaze is a symptom of the country's growing waste crisis.

stories.headline: What's at stake in Philippines' vice-president impeachment trial?
stories.summary: The political deathmatch between the country's ruling dynasties enters a critical new theatre.

stories.headline: Are Argentina being treated favourably at World Cup?
stories.summary: Egypt have alleged bias in favour of Argentina and favouritism towards their star player, Lionel Messi. Is this fair?

stories.headline: Microsoft’s gaming strategy has misfired badly
stories.summary: A supply-chain crisis for Xbox couldn’t have come at a worse time

stories.headline: Ex-US judge spared from prison for helping migrant evade federal agents
stories.summary: The 67-year-old was arrested for ushering a suspect out a courtroom side door to avoid immigration agents.

stories.headline: Maine voters, once loyal to Platner, abandon him after sexual assault allegation
stories.summary: Some voters said they stuck with the progressive candidate through several controversies, but drew the line after a new allegation of sex assault.

stories.headline: Zelensky presses Nato for air defence systems after intense Russian strikes
stories.summary: The Ukrainian president says "decisions for air defence" should be "one of the key outcomes" of this week's summit in Turkey.

stories.headline: Mbappe condemns racist posts from Paraguay senator
stories.summary: France forward Kylian Mbappe condemns a Paraguayan senator as "despicable" and unworthy of her position after she made a number of racist remarks mocking his origin and education.

stories.headline: What will define Elon Musk’s legacy? Doge cuts to USAID Ebola programs
stories.summary: Experts say cuts have hindered the response to DRC’s Ebola outbreak and resulted in ‘significant numbers’ of deathsElon Musk has an Ebola problem. SpaceX stock dropped precipitously after its initial public offering, and Tesla faces a wave of lawsuits. But instead of focusing on his companies, Musk has posted frequently on X about the US Agency for International Development (USAID), which he helped dismantle – or, in his words, feed into the wood

stories.headline: Australian PM says Chinese missile test could have caused ‘considerable damage’ if weaponised

stories.headline: Catnip lotion as effective as Deet at repelling mosquitoes, study finds
stories.summary: Researchers testing a cheap, homegrown oil in Uganda found what cats knew all along – it worked as well as the artificial chemical used globally A homegrown catnip lotion has proven “just as effective as Deet” as a mosquito repellant in trials carried out in Uganda.Catnip, or Nepeta cataria, is a common herb from the mint family. The chemical in the plant that causes feline euphoria – nepetalactone – also has insect-repelling properties but this

stories.headline: Russian attack leaves gaping hole in Kyiv residential building
stories.summary: The BBC's Sarah Rainsford is at the scene of one of the blasts to hit Ukraine's capital overnight.

stories.headline: Headteacher among four killed in deadliest Israeli attack on Lebanon since latest ceasefire
stories.summary: Three women and one man were travelling in a car after visiting their family home, Lebanese state media reported.

stories.headline: Huge crowds fill Tehran streets for Khamenei's funeral procession
stories.summary: Many people were waving Iranian flags and red banners symbolising vengeance for the former supreme leader's war death.

stories.summary: Tropical storm causes extreme flooding in south of the country with heavy rainfall expected in coming daysA tropical storm has killed two people, caused dam breaches and forced tens of thousands to evacuate in southern China.Typhoon Maysak killed two people in Nanning, in China’s southern Guangxi province. stories.headline: Iran's supreme leader absent as senior officials attend ayatollah's funeral
stories.summary: Mojtaba Khamenei has not been seen in public since the attack which killed his father at the outset of the US-Israel war with Iran.

stories.headline: A global hub for fake luxury goods, Vietnam cracks down on its black market
stories.summary: The Trump administration wants Vietnam to stamp out its booming counterfeit industry. Locals are divided.

stories.headline: Keiko Fujimori declared winner of Peru's presidential election weeks after vote
stories.summary: The daughter of disgraced president Alberto Fujimori is declared winner of tight race nearly a month after voting took place.

stories.headline: Iran nuclear and military damage revealed after restricted satellite images released
stories.summary: Targets hit include ballistic missile infrastructure, nuclear sites, and naval bases.

stories.headline: 'Two weeks after her death I got a call': Gaza patients face agonising delays for evacuation
stories.summary: An estimated 300 Palestinians referred for treatment abroad have died since the ceasefire began, according to Gaza's Hamas-run health ministry.

stories.headline: Aunt of Venezuelan boy pulled from rubble tells BBC she will give him 'mother's warmth'
stories.summary: Kleiber Moran, 2, was rescued on Wednesday, six days after last week's deadly earthquakes.

stories.headline: The US deported them to Venezuela - hours later earthquakes struck
stories.summary: They landed on Flight 164 just before the twin quakes. Family members are desperately searching for them.

stories.headline: Why the expected fight over the North American trade deal never kicked off
stories.summary: The US has confirmed it will not extend the agreement for another 16 years, but stopped short of more dramatic action.
